briefing.headline: Rig up a 10" Worm — steady pre-spawn approach for clear water.
briefing.gameplan:
The 10" Worm is a strong choice for pre-spawn with clear water and stable conditions.

For Yamamoto 10-inch grub: use Gamakatsu EWG Superline 5/0 hook with screw-in 1/4oz weight (F rig) and delayed hookset. Straight shank hooks cause missed fish

Key adjustment for stable: moderate cadence — match the natural pace of local forage.

primaryApproach.retrieve: For Yamamoto 10-inch grub: use Gamakatsu EWG Superline 5/0 hook with screw-in 1/4oz weight (F rig) and delayed hookset. Straight shank hooks cause missed fish
primaryApproach.targets: Focus on grass, creek channel, flat. Work the transitions and edges where structure changes.

alternateApproach.retrieve: Palaniuk: 'Start with a Texas-rigged 10-inch worm in brushpiles because the heavier line and bigger hook up the odds of landing fish in cover, then switch to Neko rig for more bites'
alternateApproach.targets: Focus on grass, creek channel, flat. Work the transitions and edges where structure changes.

proInsights.insight: Palaniuk: 'The spot had a riprap bank with a steep drop, then a trough that came up onto a hard bottom ridge, tapered back off into another trough. The trough made a horseshoe. A 40-yard section where the fish wanted to live, with a 25-foot hole t...

Wheeler: On flat fisheries bass stay in the shallow zone a long time before spawning, but on highland reservoirs they may stage in 6-10 feet before moving up; crankbait selection should match the lake type and depth zone.

KVD: In cold/post-frontal pre-spawn, don't just wind the Thunder Cricket. Work it like a jig — hop, twitch, pull-and-stop. Get it down in the grass and on the bottom. Bites are extremely subtle: the bait just gets heavy or the vibration stops. 'I ...

briefing.depthStrategy: Stable pre-spawn conditions mean fish are staging predictably at 5-12ft on points, channel swings, and riprap banks. They're feeding aggressively as water warms. Work the transition zone between deep winter haunts and shallow spawning areas.
adjustIf: If conditions suddenly shift (pressure drop, wind change), be ready to speed up your approach — transitional moments produce big bites
If water clarity decreases from wind or rain, upsize and switch to brighter colors
If bites dry up, try shaking in place for 10-15 seconds before moving — patience pays with plastics
